reviews.review_text:
[h1]  The definition of 'turning your brain off'—until a glitch makes you want to throw your keyboard at the wall.[/h1] 

[b]If you're looking for a game where you just hop into your rusty bucket of a car and drive toward the horizon with some lo-fi radio, you've found it. The loop of maintaining your car, where finding a fresh tire or a gas can feels like winning the lottery, is strangely addictive. The atmosphere of lonely roads and changing weather is top-tier.[/b]

[h3]But beware, it's not all sunshine and rainbows[/h3]

[u] Grind vs. Relax:[/u]  Sometimes the maintenance is overkill. You just want to drive, but instead, you're constantly fixing a falling exhaust or dealing with a car that drinks fuel like a tank.

[u] Technical State:[/u]  The game feels a bit "underbaked" at times. Physics can go wild—hit a small rock and you might end up in orbit.

[u] The Emptiness:[/u] While that's the point, after 10 hours you might wonder, "Is this it?" It lacks a stronger hook to keep you going other than just seeing what's over the next hill.

[b]Good game for listening to podcasts or just getting lost after work. Just be prepared to spend more time with your head under the hood than looking at the scenery.[/b]

I totally vibe with this game, And have spent many nights hunting for parts for cars with friends, However it's not without the downsides.

Pros:I like the vibe of the game, Driving shells of cars, slowly restoring them to their former glory. The new update adding the shop to buy the harder to find parts. I love the customisation the cars have as well, With paint, Neon and the many choices of exhaust mods (Including the funny one that claps), There is very little that you cannot do

Cons: While it's in Early Access, The current frame-rate is horrendous. My pc struggles at frames per maybe, even when following the recommended settings for my rig. The game is also plentyful with bugs of all sorts.



TD:DR-
It's an amazing concept for a game, and the vibe is there, But it's *heavily* unoptimised. 
Regardless, I'm 100% looking forward to seeing the final product of the game when it releases ^^

I am not sure why this game has such high ratings. 11+ hours in this is what I found.
First the tutorial is very vague and incomplete in my opinion. The driving physics are horrible all cars feel floaty and steering is sloppy.

The assault rifle only comes with 10 bullets, but the rocket launcher is infinite ammo? 

The bugs...
"Collision Bug" - dropping items on the ground sometimes shots them into the air or yeets them into the distance.
"Esc Crash" - On occasion when you want to save and exit or just pause the game the game locks up and you have to force close the game.  Typically results in losing progress.
"Graphics issue" - you constantly have to change the gamma settings to be able to see at all. One moment the game will be so bright and washed out you have to decrease the gamma, the next the game is so dark you have to increase the gamma.
"Urination bug" - The button to relieve yourself doesn't always work and you have to rapidly tap until it seems to register when it does sometimes it shows that you are urinating, other times you just hear it. Also why include this as a task? There is no recourse for ignoring the function. It doesn't do anything environmentally when you do, no benefit to it that I have found, you can however urinate into containers.... 
"Roof rack bug" - You can give your vehicle a roof rack and stack things on it. But as you play and add more things to the rack... you will be met with a bug where you can't remove things from it. You also will encounter a issue where if you try to remove the rack to reorganize it or swap vehicles it will clip through the map, or start lifting you in to the sky or send you into an uncontrollable spiral.

QOL
There is no details or information when you pick up parts, so you never know what you have until you store it in the "Horizon Vault". The damage, corrosion of parts over time seems extremely rapid. Every ten miles in game you want to check the condition of your parts. There are lights on the dash that give you indications of something... but none of it is labeled, and the immediate one I learned was the hand brake. Also it doesn't feel like it's scalable, like a better/bigger engine wears down just as fast as the smaller ones. The only effect an engine size seems to have is the speed at which a vehicle travels, and honestly higher speeds make it easier to wreck your vehicle because the driving controls are so squirrelly. 

It's still listed as early access, as of 5/25/26 but game was supposed to be fully released 3/24/25. Definintely missed their mark there.

The concept is amazing and when the game works you can have a lot of fun. But the game just doesnt work, there are countless game breaking bugs which make the game unplayable. I have reasonably good pc but I was forced to put my graphic settings to low to get about 25 fps. When my friend joins the game I cant even see him walking, he just glitches around the map.

In conclusion, the game needs a lot of work before its worth a 25 euro price tag.

I've been a long time fan and player of TLD consisting of well over 800 hours between my current steam account and my old steam account. I lost faith in TLD when Genesz the developer abandoned the game three years ago. I was reluctant to try DBH because I'm a stubborn a$$hole. I finally caved in last night and downloaded the demo for DBH, I was so impressed I bought the full game. I already have 7 hours on DBH in the first day (its my day off I'm not unemployed lol) and it is a night and day difference in the two games and I am not even talking about the graphics (those are amazing in DBH though) DBH has all the bugs I've come to love in TLD plus more, Vehicle customization is insane too. One of my favorite things about DBH compared to TLD is that cleaning your car up and putting new parts on your rig only buys you so much time (where once your car is 100% in TLD you're set for the remainder of the game minus fuel oil and coolant) I didn't want to believe it when I read it on the TLD Steam discussion but TLD did walk so DBH could run. 

Now tell me former TLD player (or average random joe looking for an amazing time killer) what in the f$ck are you waiting for? Go try the Demo see for yourself what I am talking about and then go buy the game!

(Also for the slower folks reading this TLD is The Long Drive and DBH is Drive Beyond Horizons)

That's all folks!

i really enjoy the game; it allows me to play and listen to an audio book at the same time. it's very Unstable though, for example: around 20% of the time i pause the game it freezes. which caused me to lose hours of collected parts. there are other examples, but this is the only one that truly got me to stop playing. i was about to have the IFA truck up and running, now the engine the big bed and many other components are just gone. Very disheartening and absolutely killing my dive to play. i will play it again after a break.

It's actually hilarious that this game is 25 dollars. Lower the cost to 2.50 for now and make it 5 to get some small addon dlc or something to help support it, but 25 dollars is just an impossible sell for this game. I cannot get any of my friends to play it with me at this price.

Game is complete trash. They add updates but don't fix the issues that come with such. My opinion is to not waste your time or money on this trash game. "OH, it's in early access..." Boo Hoo. The games been out for months and I paid good money for this game. Stupid that the Devs can't figure out a simple code fix. Maybe don't make games if you don't know how to write code....
this game is not optimize with mid range pc spec, i hope for next update the game more optimize, more than 1 year this game still worst performance : (
Restarted the game three times and have reinstalled it twice.  after 50km the game saves and objects quit loading in.  trash

While the game still has it's fair share of jank, it has so much variety in the way you can design vehicles, it can keep ya busy and havin fun for a long time, just sittin back listening to the engines roar and cruisin down the road, is it's own form of relaxation and therapy, the fun of seeing what you can discover at each stop, keeps you wanting to see what's at the next stop and the challenge of survival along side keeping your vehicle topped with fluids, gives you a somethin to work for as you travel, I have tons of hours in this and will continue to accumulate many more, especially with the insanity that the garage update added.
good game bad devs banning people for a year from their discord cause they say "stfu" to them 1 time and they also dont help unless u know your pc specs expecting that everyone is a nerd like them
I like the selection of vehicles, as well as the amount of customization with various parts. Could definitely use a tutorial, as I didn't really know what to do once I got started.

This review is after playing on and off for 26 hours throughout time.
The overall takeaway is this game is a very interesting one that take the love of driving to a different level. allowing for you to repair your car and discover all sorts of hidden or not so hidden landmarks filled with goodies to help you survive.

Below is a more in-depth view into the game.

1. Gaming mechanics
    Core Loop: At the start it's very interesting but late game feels repetitive trying to find parts for your cars. 8/10
    Controls: The controls feel natural but I'd personally like to see controller implemented better. 8/10
    Pacing: The core loops is short enough that the pacing is however you make it. Depending on the scenario you are playing or free roam where you're looking for specific items.  10/10

2. Story
    Characters: Not applicable   -/10
    Plot: No primary story as of yet  -/10
    Integration: again, no story  -/10

3. Technical Performance
    Bugs: Some bugs however some are intentional. like the flying ladder trick.   10/10
    Crashes: In 26 hours I've never crashed   10/10
    Optimization: Very nice optimization, even with my older laptop I could play at max settings. 10/10

4. Aesthetics
    Art Direction: Following the feeling of driving in a post apocalyptic world in a realistic way.  10/10
    Sound Design: Not the best at times and feels lonely. However that's probably intentional.   9/10
    Atmosphere: Absolutely perfect atmosphere for the pretense of this game. 10/10

5. Value
    Duration: Essentially-unending    10 /10
    Replay-ability: Multiple scenarios, custom stories and custom world settings.  10/10
    Quality: Very nice with lots of work over the years, still room for improvement 8/10

6. Personal Notes
    Expectations vs Reality: Coming in I expected to just drive and maintain a vehicle. I was happy to come across enemies and the reality of having to build your vehicle from the scraps of itself. [spoiler] once you finish restoring your car you can select it as your starting vehicle or save the progress in the dispensers across saves[/spoiler]  10/10
    Noticeable points:[spoiler]Ufos, Zombies, cleaning and repairing cars all very pleasant[/spoiler]   10/10
    Frustrations: When i first started little of the controls were in English, over time it has been improved but not all of them yet.   6/10

Overall score: 139/150

Updates have killed the game for me. I used to play with a combination of a mouse and an xbox controller. An update 6 months ago killed the x axis control on all 3 of my controllers. As soon as I connect one of my controllers the game starts turning right, I tried every calibration suggestion I could find. My controllers work perfectly on every other steam game I play. They only fail on this game. The devs royally screwed something up

This game has issues. Bugs that persist even after numerous updates. The game maxes out my graphics card rendering the game a 10 fps. Even at potato settings. There are too many objects being rendered. The memory leak issue. Starter car, and other cars logic breaking for achievements. There was great potential in this game. Not likely to get better. Feature stuffing which caused more frame drops. Autosave is the games worst enemy. Too many saves lose their car due to it. Radio is based on your framerate, so poor frames makes it choppier then necessary. There are good things. Game engine jank to be more vertical movement. The car types and the restoring of them. But mixed at best building types. Thus I can't recommend it to anyone. Even if you have high end hardware. Maybe there will be better games made that learn from this mess.

its i decent concept and the game is absolutely lovely but until the game developers stop adding ♥♥♥♥ and instead use the time to fix all the lagging and glitching issues where you can be fine one minute and then its like its having trouble loading ♥♥♥♥ in its not a great game at all especially when it takes like a half hour just to pass a gas station and ♥♥♥♥ hasn't loaded in yet but oh a few days ago i started having the problem after playing for a few hours and there wasn't much to it was fun till it happened but now an update comes out and all of a sudden we got weather now why are they adding stuff and not fixing stuff i get its early access and understand its all about fixing issues but when you care more about adding stuff and not addressing problems then its a problem!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Yeah. Fun game. Physics engine works 90% of the time. good graphics. random free duplication glitches. runs decently. Good car radoi. quality updates. amazing choice of cars. 9.9/10 amazing survuval car game. PS, I really like the car survival games like this or The Long Drive(The Long Drive is why the physics engine part is up there.) if you look at the hours, 613 hours is 25.5 days.
Fun game, great solo or with friends. Still pretty janky but it adds to it's charm

This is an addicting game, I really love the concept of starting with a run down car that you have to put together along the road, and it is fun to just attach random accessories to your car. the game is still in early access with many bugs and broken features, but it is still worth buying. I would love to see more content like weather, biomes, more cars, and etc. in the future. Just know that multiplayer is extremely buggy and has it's problems, please don't judge this game solely by multiplayer standards, it is an excellent solo experience too.
very good game but lag a lil bit probably cause i need to upgrade my monitor

I've played this countless times with so many friends! The goofy chaos that strives from this game is unique and provides a good base for exploration and friendship building!

Are there bugs? Sure there are. Its a game there's gonna always be bugs somewhere. Placement may be tricky when placing pedals sure, but regardless nonetheless this game is absolutely amazing and always coming out with new things! Their most recent massive update with garages, economy, etc. really brought me back and added an entirely new feeling and meaning to the game! Can't wait to see what else these developers have in store. :D
The game is fun and enjoyable, but it needs refinement and a better UI.

I part out cars and send them to the garage, then take the boxed items and shelve them for future use or for sale. Ive already filled up all shelving in the garage so far, but i need more. I think it would add a new direction for gameplay. Actually it would be cool for us to be able to have our own store to be able to sell to other players that cant be bothered to hunt down their own parts. just my random thoughts.

Can't play with friends because of hackers, passwords or lobby limits do nothing. Would recommend in the event this issue is resolved.

This game, even after months of not playing, still is able to completely brick my computer. It crashes everything so horribly bad every time I play, that I can't even hold the power button to turn my computer off: I instead have to unplug it from the wall. I don't know what these developers are smoking. The gameplay is actually fun but the game runs like s##t and has tons of bugs and crashes. These guys made hundreds of thousands of $$$$$$, early access isnt an excuse, i could remake this on roblox with less issues and better performance in 1-2 months so what is stopping y'all, a full indie team, from doing what needs to be done? I can't even watch myself piss on my friends. It seems like almost every system in the game has a bug. 9/10 Gameplay, but 4/10 Overall for all the issues with it. If they ever fix all the critical bugs and performance then I will change this review and recommend it

Good Day everyone. First things, I really liked the ability to find parts and maintain your vehicle and just  improve it. While there are a lot if issues with places not spawning in correctly, or completely, some are just empty. Parts for some cars only appear  when that car spawned was annoying, meaning you cant find parts for certain cars unless they spawn randomly on the map or in a garage. There is so much room for improvement but I recommend the game for it is just fun, albeit it can be annoying.

I want to like this game, but Idk what the Devs are smoking not prioritizing dedicated self hosted multiplayer servers over anything else. 

P2P multiplayer ruins the game, riddled with bugs kind of like the rest of the game.

Stop adding things until the core mechanics aren't such a mess, refinement is needed in so many areas before adding more trash.

So much potential to make a great gaming experience and seems to be squandered by poor choices and lack of prioritization/skill in general.
Infuriatingly fun, simple, clean and easy game, that I often find my self losing time in.
Cannot explain why it is fun collecting car parts at the same locations over and over again, but they got me hooked. Glitches are ridiculous and fun.

Could have better optimization, but if we can look away from that. It's a good game, support is always there and helps a lot, they've made it known that updates that offer better performance are OTW so hopefully those help. Multiplayer has some bugs that are funny, some that aren't and performance in multiplayer is a LOT worse than Single-player. If you're looking to get this game for a multiplayer aspect and you have a low-mid range PC, I'd say wait to be honest. But if you're looking to play single-player just to chill and don't mind medium-low settings, I'd say its a great game to get and play. if I had to rate this game at its current state id give it a 6/10 but from what the community has said is coming seems like that rating is gonna go up.
Cheaters ruin your grind by joining with ray guns and destroying everything you have.

I waited a YEAR before i bought this game for 24.99 and it was not worth it i expected after a year and with feedback from the game that it would get its issues smoothed out but it seems that isn't the case i don't usually write reviews but this game is unplayable every session i play i run into glitches/bugs consistently i don't mind the repetitiveness but every time i run into an issue like poi's not spawning anything or loading in to find my vehicle completely missing its disheartening because the concept is there but it honestly feels like a product that gets worse over time i read all of the issues with the game in advance hoping the the patches resolved some of them but it didn't it feels like they want to add more content rather than fixing what's broken i would not recommend buying this game its a 10$ game at best its choppy lag spikes consistently and if you try to recover any back ups it'll duplicate your vehicle on top of each other resulting in collisions that make your vehicle unusable and makes you restart your progress they also didn't label parts correctly leaving you confused trying to find out where a headlight goes when the whole time it was an interior light the game is an incomplete mess that will leave you feeling frustrated and scammed i rate it a 1/10 the concept is there but the foundation isn't.
Don`t buy it yet. There is nothing in this game for now. If devs decide to actually build a full game out of this, I will change my review, but it smells like another quick cahsgrab.

All this game needs is steering wheel/shifter support like the g29 and it would be one of the best driving games out there with the survival aspect but for now i wouldn't be encouraging others to play it unless they do since most of my friends play driving games with steering wheel

I have been having an absolute blast playing Drive Beyond Horizons. It is an incredibly enjoyable experience that completely hooks you from the start. If you love driving games with great progression and immersive gameplay, this one is definitely worth adding to your Steam library.

Considering that, it was fun for the first hour when you're figuring things out but as soon as you hit the road then it becomes a repeatitive thing. It lacks a good map, gameplay is trash, optimization is poor. Overall it lacks the content and it shouldn't be in Early Access, Beta testing at max. I tried to like it but couldn't. Sorry not sorry.

Honestly, this game is great. It has slowly become a comfort game for me where after a long day at work I can sit back with a drink, cruise, tinker on my cars, and enjoy the thrills of RNG doing me dirty.... 

Quips aside; Yes, there are some elements that do need work (mostly just a good polish...car pun absolutely intended) like mild performance issues in multiplayer, physics related issues, occasionally clunky or confusing control schemes and/or odd initial key binds, and weird quirks (we all got'em) such as trying to wire brush the truck roll cage thing that has two lights on top (the exact name escapes me but that's the closest I've got, deal with it), but the dev team feels dedicated and invested, seemingly at a personal level, in the growth of the community they have created.

I've only got ~31 hours in at this point but I've had the game for almost a year now. To witness the improvements that the dev team has made and the community communication they do on their discord is a super encouraging thing to see as a consumer.

Keep up the awesome work!
If you want fun with friends or whatever, guys... that game ? Really, really, TOP!

A good base game with lots of possible upgrades

For 25$ you get:

A garage cleanup and order fulfilment simulator
A car parts and junk foraging simulator
An 'I didn't take care of my car and now I'm walking down a road that uses the metric system and am now dying under the sun in Celsius rather than Fahrenheit' simulator
A really roundabout way to experience the Backrooms.
A Zombie Apocalypse progressive roguelike
A unique Tetris experience (No, really. You will spend a lot of time with this aspect)
Pugs that will not shut up until they do, and then don't again. Abandon them to their annoying fate.
A Pet Rock collectathon! (there are four)
An untold amount of eyes constantly following you wherever you go (I'm watching you right back, fish)


AND

A handful of 'possible' cars that are highly customizable and fun to drive and display at your garage so long as you don't mind waiting half an hour for the Garage to load.

That's, like, at least one thing per dollar spent. And that's not bad.

Spent 3 hours looking for one car and another 2 hours searching for wheels. after discovering a trampoloine i finally jumped on a roof to find a new set i wanted.... 

10/10 would do it again

This game is a total joke. 

They keep saying they're rolling out updates, but the bugs are piling up, to the point where it's impossible to play! All my saved games are completely glitched: some pieces float in the air while remaining “glued” to the car, and when I try to remove them, they get stuck with my pointer, impossible to release or replace with anything else. Only solution is to quit the game.
This is a total joke and a lack of respect for players who are just trying to play their game. 
Right now, this game is unplayable, disappointing, and ridiculously buggy. 

Keep moving on, you who are wondering whether you should buy this game. Don’t waste your time, money, and energy only to end up with your progress completely ruined and impossible to recover. There are much better games out there, at a more affordable price.

One word : disappointing.
A great fun concept but too many bugs, after a while there are finally some nice updates but items keep glitching in the ground or cars flying away...
this is the jankiest and buggiest game ive ever played but the gameplay is fun
